title: Zornia nutans
intro: Zornia nutans is a species of legume native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its annual or perennial growth habit and distinctive clusters of purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Zornia nutans include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Zornia nutans is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to make teas and tinctures that are used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Zornia nutans is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas and is a member of the genus Zornia, which consists of over 100 species of annual and perennial herbs.
